**Column definitions (COLUMN_DEFS):** 9 columns defined — Time, Sentiment, Source, Headline, Summary, Ticker, Confidence, Risk Level, Tradeable. Each has defaultVisible, required (Headline cannot be toggled off), and requiredFeature for tier gating.

Locked columns show a padlock SVG icon, toggle is disabled, and column is hidden from the table. Sentiment requires 'sentiment_filter' (locked for Free). {
  "project": "InstantNews Terminal Evolution",
  "branchName": "ralph/terminal-evolution",
  "description": "Transform the SIGNAL terminal into a competitive financial intelligence platform: sync AI backfill to production, fix ticker recommendation pipeline, add dynamic column customization, integrate Polygon.io real-time market data, and build comprehensive company profiles with SEC EDGAR institutional/insider data.",
  "userStories": [
    {
      "id": "US-001",
      "title": "Export Local AI Backfill Data to SQL Dump",
      "description": "As a developer, I want to export all AI-analyzed article data from my local database so that I can import it into production RDS.",
      "acceptanceCriteria": [
        "Script scripts/export_ai_backfill.py exports AI fields (sentiment_score, sentiment_label, target_asset, asset_type, confidence, risk_level, tradeable, reasoning, ai_analyzed) for all locally analyzed articles",
        "Export format is a SQL file with UPDATE statements keyed on article link (unique identifier)",
        "Script reports count of exported records",
        "Handles NULL values correctly in generated SQL",
        "Typecheck passes"
      ],
      "priority": 1,
      "notes": "First step of DB sync — must complete before US-002",
      "test_strategy": "cli",
      "test_assertions": [
        "python scripts/export_ai_backfill.py --dry-run exits 0 and prints record count",
        "python scripts/export_ai_backfill.py --output /tmp/ai_backfill.sql exits 0",
        "test -f /tmp/ai_backfill.sql exits 0",
        "grep -c 'UPDATE news' /tmp/ai_backfill.sql returns count > 0",
        "mypy scripts/export_ai_backfill.py exits 0"
      ],
      "status": "TEST_PASSED",
      "passes": true,
      "implementation_notes": "Created scripts/export_ai_backfill.py that queries all ai_analyzed=True articles from local DB and generates a SQL dump with UPDATE statements keyed on article link. Supports --dry-run (prints count + preview) and --output <path> (writes SQL file). SQL output wraps in BEGIN/COMMIT transaction. Handles NULL values, boolean conversion (TRUE/FALSE), float formatting, and single-quote escaping in text fields. Also fixed mypy typing in app/database.py (dict[str, Any] for kwargs) and added [tool.mypy] ignore_missing_imports config to pyproject.toml so mypy passes cleanly on the script.",
      "files_changed": ["scripts/export_ai_backfill.py", "app/database.py", "pyproject.toml"],
      "failure_reason": null,
      "test_evidence": "All 5 test assertions passed. See ralph/test_results/US-001/attempt-1/ for full artifacts. --dry-run: exits 0, prints 'Found 31067 AI-analyzed articles to export' (dryrun_stdout.log). --output: exits 0, creates 40MB SQL file with 31067 UPDATE statements (output/ai_backfill.sql). All 9 AI fields present in UPDATE SET clauses. SQL wraps in BEGIN/COMMIT, single quotes properly escaped as ''. mypy: 'Success: no issues found in 1 source file' (mypy_stdout.log). pytest: 86 passed, 8 failed (all 8 are pre-existing test_tiers.py/test_rate_limit.py failures unrelated to US-001).",
      "test_report": "## US-001 Test Report — PASSED\n\n### Test Assertions\n1. PASS: `--dry-run` exits 0, prints 'Found 31067 AI-analyzed articles to export'\n2. PASS: `--output <path>` exits 0, creates SQL file\n3. PASS: Output file exists (40MB, 37744 lines)\n4. PASS: `grep -c 'UPDATE news'` returns 31067\n5. PASS: `mypy` exits 0 — 'Success: no issues found in 1 source file'\n\n### Acceptance Criteria\n1. PASS: Exports all 9 AI fields (sentiment_score, sentiment_label, target_asset, asset_type, confidence, risk_level, tradeable, reasoning, ai_analyzed)\n2. PASS: SQL UPDATE statements keyed on article link — 31067 statements, all with `WHERE link = '...'`\n3. PASS: Reports count — dry-run shows 31067, export confirms 31067\n4. PASS: NULL handling — source DB has 0 NULL AI fields; SQL format correctly handles empty strings as ''\n5. PASS: mypy clean\n\n### Quality Checks\n- mypy: clean\n- pytest: 86/94 passed; 8 pre-existing failures in test_tiers.py/test_rate_limit.py (stale tier config values, not related to US-001)\n\n### SQL Validation\n- Starts with `BEGIN;`, ends with `COMMIT;`\n- 31067 UPDATE statements matching 31067 source records\n- Single-quote escaping verified ('' used throughout reasoning fields)\n- Boolean values: TRUE/FALSE (PostgreSQL-compatible)\n- Float formatting: decimal notation (e.g., 0.0, 0.35)",
      "fail_count": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "US-002",
      "title": "Import AI Backfill Data into Production RDS",
      "description": "As a developer, I want to safely import the AI backfill SQL dump into production PostgreSQL so that existing articles gain ticker recommendations.",
      "acceptanceCriteria": [
        "Script scripts/import_ai_backfill.py reads the SQL dump and executes against target DATABASE_URL",
        "Runs in a single transaction with rollback on any error",
        "--dry-run flag parses and validates SQL without executing",
        "Reports: matched articles updated, unmatched articles skipped, errors encountered",
        "Does NOT overwrite articles that already have ai_analyzed=True in production (skip those)",
        "Typecheck passes"
      ],
      "priority": 2,
      "notes": "Depends on US-001. Prod test required: run --dry-run against production DATABASE_URL first, then actual import.",
      "test_strategy": "cli",
      "test_assertions": [
        "python scripts/import_ai_backfill.py --dry-run --input /tmp/ai_backfill.sql exits 0 and prints summary",
        "Output includes 'matched', 'skipped', 'errors' counts",
        "mypy scripts/import_ai_backfill.py exits 0"
      ],
      "status": "TEST_PASSED",
      "passes": true,
      "implementation_notes": "Created scripts/import_ai_backfill.py that reads SQL dumps produced by export_ai_backfill.py and applies them to the target DATABASE_URL. Runs in a single transaction with rollback on any error. Before executing, queries the target DB to identify articles that already have ai_analyzed=True and skips them. --dry-run mode parses the SQL file, validates all statements, and queries the target DB to report matched/skipped/unmatched/error counts without executing any updates. Handles multi-line reasoning fields and escaped single quotes. Self-validated: 31067 statements parsed with 0 errors, mypy clean, no test regressions (86 passed, 8 pre-existing failures in test_tiers.py/test_rate_limit.py).",

Real import: exits 0, commits transaction, updated=0, skipped=31067 (all already had ai_analyzed=True) — confirms skip logic works (real_import_stdout.log).       "test_report": "## US-002 Test Report — PASSED\n\n### Test Assertions\n1. PASS: `--dry-run --input <path>` exits 0, prints summary with 31067 parsed statements\n2. PASS: Output includes 'matched' (2 occurrences), 'skipped' (1), 'errors' (3)\n3. PASS: `mypy scripts/import_ai_backfill.py` exits 0 — 'Success: no issues found in 1 source file'\n\n### Acceptance Criteria\n1. PASS: Script reads SQL dump and executes against target DATABASE_URL — real import run completed (exit 0, committed)\n2. PASS: Single transaction with rollback on error — code uses try/except/rollback pattern; real run committed successfully\n3. PASS: --dry-run parses and validates without executing — 31067 statements parsed, counts reported, no DB writes\n4. PASS: Reports matched/skipped/unmatched/errors — all counts present in both dry-run and real output\n5. PASS: Does NOT overwrite ai_analyzed=True articles — 31067 matched, 31067 skipped, 0 updated\n6. PASS: Typecheck passes — mypy clean\n\n### Additional Validation\n- Malformed SQL input: parser correctly ignores invalid lines, reports unmatched articles\n- Nonexistent article links: correctly reported as 'unmatched'\n\n### Quality Checks\n- mypy: clean\n- pytest: 86/94 passed; 8 pre-existing failures in test_tiers.py/test_rate_limit.py (stale tier config values, documented in progress.txt)",
      "fail_count": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "US-003",
      "title": "Diagnose and Fix Production AI Worker Pipeline",
      "description": "As a developer, I want to verify that the production worker correctly triggers AI analysis on new articles so that ticker recommendations are generated automatically going forward.",
      "acceptanceCriteria": [
        "Audit app/services/feed_refresh.py _run_bedrock_analysis() — confirm it's called after feed fetch",

        "Verify production ECS task definition includes all required AI env vars: BEDROCK_ENABLED, MINIMAX_API_KEY, MINIMAX_BASE_URL, MINIMAX_MODEL_ID, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY",
